enriched_points.expanded_content: Plasmonic materials significantly enhance electron transfer rates in batteries, improving redox reactions and overall performance, as demonstrated by recent studies.
The incorporation of localized surface plasmon resonance in supercapacitors accelerates electrochemical reactions, leading to higher energy storage efficiency and faster charge/discharge cycles.
Utilizing plasmonic nanostructures results in batteries and supercapacitors with extended lifespans, minimizing degradation and maintaining performance over extended usage periods.
Plasmonic materials can be tailored for various energy applications, from batteries to supercapacitors, showcasing their adaptability in enhancing energy storage technologies.
